mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, consumer spending collapses and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signal synchronized private demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to yield a positive return frequency above 52% over 20-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth yields negative 60-day returns in greater than 60% of expansionary periods or Rank IC drops below 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening labor demand and wage growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive payroll revisions correlate with negative 5-day returns in greater than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 215.0, inflation expectations accelerate and force tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 215.0 coincide with positive 20-day returns in greater than 58% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Combined GDPC1 and RSAFS growth fails to produce a positive return frequency above 50% over 60-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals abrupt supply-side contraction and inventory destocking, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO drops greater than 1.0% coincide with positive 20-day returns in greater than 55% of instances.
